The anterior two-thirds of the tongue receives its primary sensory innervation via both the facial nerve (VII), specifically through the chorda tympani branch, and the trigeminal nerve (CN V). Additionally, the lingual nerve, derived from the mandibular division of CN V, further enhances the sensory input to this area.
